question: How can you adjust the rear-shock assemblies for a lawn mower with suspension system only?
content:
On machines with the suspension system, adjust the two rear-shock assemblies to set ride comfort. The slot guide marks position 1 as firmest, position 2 as softest, and the detents as reference points only; the assemblies can be set anywhere in the slots.<PIC>
1. Unlock the rear-shock assembly latch.
2. Slide the assembly in its slot to the desired ride position.
3. Adjust the left and right rear-shock assemblies to the same position.
4. Lock the latch after setting the position; the adjustment graphic shows the correct matched and locked arrangement.<PIC>
ret:
"On machines with the suspension system, adjust the two rear-shock assemblies to set ride comfort. The slot guide marks position 1 as firmest, position 2 as softest, and the detents as reference points only; the assemblies can be set anywhere in the slots.<PIC>
1. Unlock the rear-shock assembly latch.
2. Slide the assembly in its slot to the desired ride position.
3. Adjust the left and right rear-shock assemblies to the same position.
4. Lock the latch after setting the position; the adjustment graphic shows the correct matched and locked arrangement.<PIC>",["Manual23_37", "Manual23_38"]
notes: Grounded in the Toro Z Master 4000 suspension-only rear-shock adjustment section and its adjacent figures. Images show the soft/firm detent reference positions and the latch/slot adjustment sequence.
